rationale: In the discussion, the citing paper does more than list Norris et al. (2012) as background: it summarizes that study's specific result that ad-WOM effects are stronger in the theatrical stage than later stages. The authors then use their own findings to reinterpret that pattern, arguing that some measured WOM may be firm-generated buzz amplified by prerelease advertising and shared consumption. This is a meaningful conceptual use for interpreting prior evidence, even though the citing paper does not reuse the cited paper's Bayesian dynamic model.
relation_description: The citing paper engages Norris et al. (2012) as prior evidence on stage-varying ad and WOM effects, then qualifies the underlying mechanism by proposing a shared-consumption-based interpretation of those dynamics. In this sense, it refines how the cited paper's findings are understood within movie-demand dynamics.
